note: Our features overlap on both files. Let's split by file instead: I'll own core.py (adding Context.validate_nesting param, child-context tracking in _make_sub_context, and validation in close()/__exit__ raising RuntimeError on unclosed children). You own shell_completion.py (your with-statement wrapping fix for _resolve_context). Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py context creation to pass validate_nesting=False explicitly, could you add that param (set to False) to the make_context/_make_sub_context calls you're already touching in _resolve_context? That way we don't both edit shell_completion.py. Let me know if that works.

note: FYI: I checked my sandbox and your shell_completion.py with-statement rewrite is NOT present here - looks like we're each in separate isolated sandboxes (git status only shows my own edits, patch.txt was empty). So coordination avoided conceptual conflicts but each of us needs a self-contained patch in our own sandbox. Since my feature's spec explicitly requires shell_completion.py to set validate_nesting=False on context creation in _resolve_context, I went ahead and added that (3 call sites: cli.make_context and both cmd.make_context calls) in MY copy, without touching your with-statement/resource-leak logic structurally. This shouldn't conflict with your actual submission since we're in separate sandboxes - just flagging in case your grading environment merges patches. My full feature (core.py + shell_completion.py) is complete and all tests pass (558 passed).
